title: Extend TodoList with search and sort pipeline
description:
## What

Replace the single-step completion filter in TodoList with a composable three-step pipeline: completion filter → search filter → sort. Also update the empty-state message and animation key.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `searchQuery` and `sortOrder` destructured from `useUIStore()` alongside existing `filter`
- [ ] Three-step pipeline applied in order: completion → search → sort
- [ ] Search is case-insensitive substring match on `title`
- [ ] Sort: `created_desc` preserves DB array order (return 0), `due_asc`/`due_desc` sort by due_date timestamp with nulls at bottom
- [ ] `[...afterSearch].sort()` spread used — the original array is NEVER mutated
- [ ] Empty state message shows `No todos match \\` when searchQuery is non-empty
- [ ] AnimatePresence/empty-state key updated to `key={filter + searchQuery}`
- [ ] All 9 combinations (3 completion filters × 3 sort states) behave correctly
- [ ] `tsc --noEmit` passes

## Technical Notes

**File:** `src/components/todo-list.tsx`

**Step 1 — Update the useUIStore destructure:**
```ts
const { filter, searchQuery, sortOrder } = useUIStore()
```

**Step 2 — Replace the filter pipeline:**
```ts
// Step 1: completion filter (existing — unchanged)
const afterFilter = initialTodos.filter((todo) => {
  if (filter === 'active') return !todo.completed
  if (filter === 'completed') return !!todo.completed
  return true
})

// Step 2: search filter (new)
const trimmedQuery = searchQuery.trim().toLowerCase()
const afterSearch = trimmedQuery
  ? afterFilter.filter((todo) =>
      todo.title.toLowerCase().includes(trimmedQuery)
    )
  : afterFilter

// Step 3: sort (new) — ALWAYS use spread to avoid mutating initialTodos prop
const filteredTodos = [...afterSearch].sort((a, b) => {
  if (sortOrder === 'created_desc') return 0  // preserve DB order

  const aDate = a.due_date ? new Date(a.due_date).getTime() : null
  const bDate = b.due_date ? new Date(b.due_date).getTime() : null

  // Nulls go to bottom regardless of direction
  if (aDate === null && bDate === null) return 0
  if (aDate === null) return 1
  if (bDate === null) return -1

  return sortOrder === 'due_asc' ? aDate - bDate : bDate - aDate
})
```

**Step 3 — Update empty state message:**
```ts
const emptyMessage = searchQuery.trim()
  ? `No todos match "${searchQuery}"`
  : EMPTY_STATE_COPY[filter]  // (or however the existing copy is referenced)
```

**Step 4 — Update empty state animation key:**
Change the `key` prop on the empty-state `m.div` from `key={filter}` to:
```tsx
key={filter + searchQuery}
```
This ensures Framer Motion re-mounts the element when either filter or search changes.

**CRITICAL:** The `sortOrder === 'created_desc'` fast-path returning `0` relies on `initialTodos` already being sorted by `created_at DESC` from the DB query. This is the existing behavior in `src/app/page.tsx` (`orderBy(desc(todos.created_at))`) — do not change that query.
close_reason: Added three-step filter/search/sort pipeline to TodoList: completion filter -> case-insensitive search filter -> sort by created_desc/due_asc/due_desc with nulls-at-bottom. Updated empty-state message and animation key to include searchQuery. tsc passes with no errors.

title: Date filter pipeline — Today view + date picker
description:
# Date Filter Pipeline — Today View + Date Picker

## Track: A — BlueLake
## Scope: `src/store/ui.ts`, `src/components/todo-list.tsx`, new `src/components/date-filter-bar.tsx`

## Description
Add a date-based filter dimension so users can view todos due today or on any specific past date.

## Implementation

### 1. Zustand store (`src/store/ui.ts`)
Add to the UIState interface:
```ts
dateFilter: null | 'today' | string  // null = all todos, 'today' = today, ISO date = specific date
setDateFilter: (v: null | 'today' | string) => void
```
Add to initial state: `dateFilter: null`
Add setter: `setDateFilter: (v) => set({ dateFilter: v })`
Export updated type.

### 2. TodoList pipeline (`src/components/todo-list.tsx`)
Add Step 0 BEFORE the existing completion filter:
```ts
const { filter, searchQuery, sortOrder, dateFilter } = useUIStore()
const today = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)

// Step 0: date filter
const afterDate = dateFilter === null
  ? initialTodos
  : initialTodos.filter(todo => {
      if (!todo.due_date) return false
      const dueDatePart = todo.due_date.slice(0, 10) // "YYYY-MM-DD"
      const targetDate = dateFilter === 'today' ? today : dateFilter
      return dueDatePart === targetDate
    })

// Step 1: completion filter (use afterDate instead of initialTodos)
const afterFilter = afterDate.filter(todo => { ... })
```
Update AnimatePresence key: `key={filter + searchQuery + (dateFilter ?? '')}`

Empty state for date filter: "No todos due on [formatted date]" when dateFilter is set and afterDate is empty.

### 3. DateFilterBar component (`src/components/date-filter-bar.tsx`)
```tsx
'use client'

interface DateFilterBarProps {
  todos: Array<{ due_date: string | null }>  // for computing today's count client-side
}
```

Layout (horizontal flex row):
- **"All" button**: `variant="ghost"` or custom, active when dateFilter===null, accent-violet when active
- **"Today" button**: active when dateFilter==='today', shows badge with count of due-today todos
- **Date input**: `<input type="date" max={today} className="glass-surface ...">` — when changed, sets dateFilter to selected date
- **Clear (×) button**: shown when dateFilter is not null, resets to null

Today count computation (client-side):
```ts
const today = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)
const todayCount = todos.filter(t => t.due_date?.slice(0, 10) === today).length
```

Styling:
- Container: `flex items-center gap-2 mb-4`
- "All" / "Today" buttons: `.glass-surface` base, `border border-[var(--color-accent-violet)] text-[var(--color-accent-violet)]` when active
- Date input: `glass-surface rounded-md px-2 py-1 text-sm border border-white/20`
- Today badge: small count chip, spectrum-2 color

## Technical Notes
- Use `<input type="date">` in DateFilterBar (NOT shadcn Calendar) — keeps Track B independent
- The beautiful DateTimePicker (Track B) is for todo due-date editing, not dashboard browsing
- `max={today}` on date input prevents future date selection (past days only)
- `useUIStore` imported from '@/store/ui'
- DateFilterBar is placed between TodoForm and SearchSortBar in page.tsx (already wired in A1)

### UI Standard
- No standard defined — follow existing component patterns in the codebase.
  (Match SearchSortBar glass-surface style; accent-violet for active state)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Zustand store exports `dateFilter` and `setDateFilter`
- [ ] "All" button shows all todos (no date filter), highlighted when active
- [ ] "Today" button filters to todos with due_date = today, shows count badge
- [ ] Date input lets user pick any past date; shows todos due on that date
- [ ] Clear (×) button resets to "All" (dateFilter = null)
- [ ] Empty state message: "No todos due on [date]" when no matches
- [ ] DateFilterBar visible in page.tsx between TodoForm and SearchSortBar
- [ ] AnimatePresence key updated to include dateFilter
- [ ] No TypeScript errors
close_reason: Added dateFilter state to Zustand store (null|today|string). Extended TodoList with Step 0 date filter pipeline. Implemented DateFilterBar with All/Today toggle buttons, date picker input, today count badge, and clear button using glass-surface styling. Updated page.tsx to pass allTodos to DateFilterBar. tsc and build pass cleanly.

title: Set up drizzle.config.ts and run initial migration
description:
Configure drizzle-kit and create the todos table in the local SQLite database.

## Steps
1. Create `drizzle.config.ts` in project root:
   ```ts
   import { defineConfig } from 'drizzle-kit'

   export default defineConfig({
     dialect: 'turso',
     schema:   './src/lib/schema.ts',
     out:      './migrations',
     dbCredentials: {
       url:       process.env.TURSO_DATABASE_URL!,
       authToken: process.env.TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN,
     },
   })
   ```
2. Add npm scripts to `package.json`:
   ```json
   "db:push":    "drizzle-kit push",
   "db:studio":  "drizzle-kit studio",
   "db:migrate": "drizzle-kit migrate"
   ```
3. Run: `npm run db:push` — this creates `local.db` with the `todos` table
4. Verify: open drizzle studio (`npm run db:studio`) and confirm todos table exists

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `drizzle.config.ts` exists with `dialect: 'turso'`
- [ ] `npm run db:push` runs without errors
- [ ] `local.db` exists in project root
- [ ] `todos` table has all 6 columns: id, title, completed, due_date, created_at, updated_at
- [ ] `local.db` is listed in `.gitignore`

## Technical Notes
- `drizzle-kit push` is for LOCAL dev only — no migration files needed for dev workflow
- For prod (Turso): run `drizzle-kit migrate` against the Turso URL before first deploy
- `local.db` MUST be in `.gitignore` — add it now if not already present
- `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN` in config: undefined is OK for local `file:` URLs — drizzle-kit handles it
- The `migrations/` directory is for prod use; commit it to git
close_reason: drizzle.config.ts created with dialect='turso', schema='./src/lib/schema.ts'. .env.local created with TURSO_DATABASE_URL=file:./local.db. drizzle-kit installed as devDependency. npx drizzle-kit push ran successfully — local.db created with todos table (id, title, completed, due_date, created_at, updated_at). Verified with sqlite3.

title: Extend Zustand store with searchQuery and sortOrder state
description:
## What

Add search query and sort order state to the Zustand UI store so all components can share these values reactively.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `searchQuery: string` field added to `UIState` interface (default `''`)
- [ ] `sortOrder: 'created_desc' | 'due_asc' | 'due_desc'` field added (default `'created_desc'`)
- [ ] `setSearchQuery(q: string)` setter added and wired in `create()` call
- [ ] `setSortOrder(s: SortOrder)` setter added and wired in `create()` call
- [ ] `SortOrder` type exported from the file for reuse in other components
- [ ] `tsc --noEmit` passes with no new errors
- [ ] Existing consumers (`FilterTabs`, `TodoList`, `TodoItem`) are not broken

## Technical Notes

**File:** `src/store/ui.ts`

This is purely additive — do not touch existing `filter`, `editingId`, `setFilter`, or `setEditingId` logic.

Add above the existing `UIState` interface:
```ts
export type SortOrder = 'created_desc' | 'due_asc' | 'due_desc'
```

Extend the interface:
```ts
interface UIState {
  // ... existing fields ...
  searchQuery: string
  sortOrder: SortOrder
  setSearchQuery: (q: string) => void
  setSortOrder: (s: SortOrder) => void
}
```

Extend the `create<UIState>` call:
```ts
searchQuery: '',
sortOrder: 'created_desc',
setSearchQuery: (q) => set({ searchQuery: q }),
setSortOrder: (s) => set({ sortOrder: s }),
```
close_reason: Added SortOrder type export, searchQuery and sortOrder state fields, and setSearchQuery/setSortOrder setters to Zustand UIStore. Purely additive - existing filter/editingId fields untouched. tsc passes.

title: Create TodoProgress component and wire into page.tsx
description:
## Context There is currently no visual indicator of overall task completion progress. This bead creates a new `TodoProgress` component showing how many todos are completed out of the total, placed between FilterTabs and TodoList. ## What to Do **Step 1: Create `src/components/todo-progress.tsx` (new file):** ```tsx 'use client' import type { Todo } from '@/lib/schema' interface TodoProgressProps { todos: Todo[] } export function TodoProgress({ todos }: TodoProgressProps) { const total = todos.length const completedCount = todos.filter((t) => !!t.completed).length const pct = total === 0 ? 0 : Math.round((completedCount / total) * 100) if (total === 0) return null return (
Progress {completedCount} / {total}
{/* CSS transition used (not Framer Motion) to avoid LazyMotion scope issues in parent */}
) } ``` **Step 2: Wire into `src/app/page.tsx`:** Add import: ```tsx import { TodoProgress } from '@/components/todo-progress' ``` Place between FilterTabs and TodoList: ```tsx ``` ## Technical Notes - `'use client'` directive is required because the component renders differently based on `todos` data (it filters on the client side) - CSS transition instead of Framer Motion: `TodoList` owns the `LazyMotion` wrapper; `TodoProgress` is rendered outside of it in page.tsx. Using Framer Motion `m.div` here would require either moving LazyMotion up to layout or adding a second LazyMotion — CSS transition avoids this entirely - `if (total === 0) return null` — hides the bar when there are no todos yet (avoids 0% bar on empty state) - `!!t.completed` coercion from integer to boolean (per AGENTS.md pattern) - `bg-accent-violet` requires Bead 1 (token registration) and Bead 2 (page.tsx already has the import location ready) ## UI Standard - Pattern: progress indicator - Standard: inline — h-1.5 track (bg-white/10), fill (bg-accent-violet), CSS width transition 0.5s ease-out, label row above with "Progress" and "N / M" count ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `src/components/todo-progress.tsx` created with correct props interface - [ ] Bar fill uses `bg-accent-violet` and width matches completion percentage - [ ] CSS transition animates width changes smoothly (not Framer Motion) - [ ] Component returns `null` when `todos.length === 0` - [ ] Correctly placed in `page.tsx` between FilterTabs and TodoList - [ ] `npm run typecheck` passes - [ ] Progress updates live when todos are toggled (RSC revalidation)
close_reason: done

title: Implement Server Actions (CRUD) with input validation
description:
Create all CRUD Server Actions for todos with proper input sanitization.

## Security: Input Validation & Injection Hardening
All user input must be validated and sanitized before reaching the database.

## Steps
1. Create `src/actions/todos.ts`:

```ts
'use server'
import { db } from '@/lib/db'
import { todos } from '@/lib/schema'
import { eq } from 'drizzle-orm'
import { revalidatePath } from 'next/cache'

// Sanitize: strip HTML tags, limit length
function sanitizeTitle(raw: string): string {
  const stripped = raw.replace(/<[^>]*>/g, '')  // strip HTML
  const trimmed = stripped.trim()
  if (!trimmed || trimmed.length > 500) throw new Error('Invalid title')
  return trimmed
}

export async function createTodo(title: string, dueDate?: string) {
  const clean = sanitizeTitle(title)
  const due = dueDate && /^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/.test(dueDate) ? dueDate : null
  await db.insert(todos).values({ title: clean, due_date: due })
  revalidatePath('/')
}

export async function toggleTodo(id: number, completed: boolean) {
  await db.update(todos)
    .set({ completed: completed ? 1 : 0, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
    .where(eq(todos.id, id))
  revalidatePath('/')
}

export async function updateTodo(id: number, title: string, dueDate?: string) {
  const clean = sanitizeTitle(title)
  const due = dueDate && /^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/.test(dueDate) ? dueDate : null
  await db.update(todos)
    .set({ title: clean, due_date: due, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
    .where(eq(todos.id, id))
  revalidatePath('/')
}

export async function deleteTodo(id: number) {
  await db.delete(todos).where(eq(todos.id, id))
  revalidatePath('/')
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `createTodo`, `toggleTodo`, `updateTodo`, `deleteTodo` all exported
- [ ] All actions marked `'use server'`
- [ ] `revalidatePath('/')` called after every mutation
- [ ] Title sanitized: HTML stripped, length validated (1–500 chars)
- [ ] `due_date` validated against ISO 8601 date regex before insert
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors

## Technical Notes
- SECURITY: `sanitizeTitle` strips `<[^>]*>` — prevents HTML injection; React escapes on render anyway but defense-in-depth
- SECURITY: Drizzle fully parameterizes all queries — no SQL injection risk
- SECURITY: `due_date` regex `/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/` rejects non-date strings
- Do NOT use `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` anywhere in the UI — use React's default escaping
- `revalidatePath('/')` invalidates Next.js cache — triggers RSC re-render with fresh data
- `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN` must never appear in this file — only via `lib/db.ts` from env
close_reason: Server Actions CRUD implemented in src/actions/todos.ts with 'use server' directive. Functions: createTodo(title, dueDate?), toggleTodo(id), updateTodo(id, title, dueDate?), deleteTodo(id). Input validation: title trimmed and max 500 chars. completed stored as 0/1 integer. revalidatePath('/') called after each mutation. TypeScript compiles without errors.

title: Create Zustand UI store
description:
Create a minimal Zustand store for client-side UI state (filter and editing state only).

## Steps
1. Install Zustand: `npm install zustand`
2. Create `src/store/ui.ts`:

```ts
import { create } from 'zustand'

type Filter = 'all' | 'active' | 'completed'

interface UIStore {
  filter: Filter
  editingId: number | null
  setFilter: (filter: Filter) => void
  setEditingId: (id: number | null) => void
}

export const useUIStore = create<UIStore>((set) => ({
  filter:      'all',
  editingId:   null,
  setFilter:   (filter)    => set({ filter }),
  setEditingId: (editingId) => set({ editingId }),
}))
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `src/store/ui.ts` exports `useUIStore`
- [ ] `filter` defaults to `'all'`
- [ ] `editingId` defaults to `null`
- [ ] TypeScript infers types correctly (no `any`)
- [ ] No todo data stored in client state (data lives in the DB, fetched by RSC)

## Technical Notes
- Intentionally minimal — no persistence (filter resets on refresh is acceptable)
- No todo CRUD in this store — server state lives in DB, refreshed via revalidatePath
- Store must be created BEFORE FilterTabs, TodoForm, and TodoItem components
  because they all import from this file

title: Deploy to Vercel with Turso production DB
description:
Set up the Turso production database and deploy the app to Vercel.

## Pre-requisites
- Turso account (free tier sufficient): https://turso.tech
- Vercel account: https://vercel.com
- GitHub repo with code pushed

## Steps

### 1. Create Turso Database
```bash
turso db create todo-app
turso db show todo-app          # Get the libsql:// URL
turso db tokens create todo-app # Get auth token
```

### 2. Run Production Migration
```bash
# Set env vars temporarily for migration
TURSO_DATABASE_URL=libsql://your-db.turso.io \
TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN=your-token \
npm run db:migrate
```

### 3. Push to GitHub
```bash
git add .
git commit -m "feat: initial todo app"
git push origin main
```

### 4. Deploy to Vercel
```bash
vercel --prod
# OR via Vercel dashboard: import GitHub repo
```

### 5. Set Vercel Environment Variables
In Vercel project settings → Environment Variables:
- `TURSO_DATABASE_URL` = `libsql://your-db.turso.io`
- `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN` = `your-token`

### 6. Trigger Redeployment
```bash
vercel --prod  # or push a new commit
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Turso DB created and migration applied (todos table exists in prod)
- [ ] Vercel project linked to GitHub repo
- [ ] `TURSO_DATABASE_URL` and `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN` set in Vercel env vars
- [ ] Production URL is accessible and fully functional
- [ ] CRUD operations work on the deployed app
- [ ] Dark mode works on production
- [ ] `.env.local` is NOT committed to git

## Technical Notes
- SECURITY: `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN` must only exist in Vercel dashboard — never in git
- `drizzle-kit migrate` (not `push`) for prod — generates and applies migration files
- The `migrations/` directory SHOULD be committed to git (version-controlled schema)
- `serverExternalPackages: ['@libsql/client']` in next.config.ts ensures Vercel
  serverless functions can import @libsql/client correctly
- Vercel free tier is sufficient for personal/portfolio use
- If Vercel deployment fails with module errors, verify serverExternalPackages is set

title: Install shadcn Calendar + create DateTimePicker component
description:
# Install shadcn Calendar + Create DateTimePicker Component

## Track: B — GreenCastle
## Scope: `src/components/ui/calendar.tsx` (new), `src/components/ui/popover.tsx` (new if missing), new `src/components/date-time-picker.tsx`

## Description
Install the shadcn Calendar component (backed by react-day-picker v9) and build a reusable `DateTimePicker` component combining a calendar popover with a time input.

## Installation
```bash
npx shadcn add calendar
npx shadcn add popover
```
These create `src/components/ui/calendar.tsx` and `src/components/ui/popover.tsx`.

## DateTimePicker component (`src/components/date-time-picker.tsx`)

### Interface
```tsx
interface DateTimePickerProps {
  value: string | null       // ISO: "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM" or "YYYY-MM-DD" or null
  onChange: (value: string | null) => void
  placeholder?: string       // default: "Pick a date..."
  className?: string
}
```

### Layout & Behavior
```
[ CalendarIcon  Feb 27, 2026, 07:00 PM  ▼ ]   ← Trigger button
     ↓ click
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ← February 2026 →                 │  ← Calendar
│  Su Mo Tu We Th Fr Sa              │
│  ...  27 ...                       │
│─────────────────────────────────────│
│  Time:  [07] : [00]                │  ← Time input (shown after date selected)
│─────────────────────────────────────│
│              [Clear date]           │  ← Only when value is set
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Implementation details
- Trigger button: `<Button variant="outline" className="glass-surface justify-start text-left font-normal w-full">`
  - Left icon: `<CalendarIcon className="mr-2 h-4 w-4 opacity-50" />`
  - Text: formatted value or placeholder (muted color)
- Popover content: `<PopoverContent className="w-auto p-3 glass-strong border border-white/20" align="start">`
- Calendar: `<Calendar mode="single" selected={selectedDate} onSelect={handleDateSelect} initialFocus />`
- Time inputs: two `<input type="number">` for hours (0-23) and minutes (0-59), or a single `<input type="time">`
- On date select from Calendar: combine with current time (default "00:00" if no time set yet)
- On time change: update time part, keep date part
- Value storage:
  - Date only: `"YYYY-MM-DD"` (when user selects date but hasn't set time yet — or keep default 00:00 and store as datetime)
  - With time: `"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM"`
- Clear button: `<Button variant="ghost" size="sm" onClick={() => { onChange(null); setOpen(false) }}>Clear date</Button>`
- Display formatting (for trigger button):
  ```ts
  function formatDisplay(value: string | null): string {
    if (!value) return placeholder
    const [datePart, timePart] = value.split('T')
    const date = new Date(datePart + 'T00:00:00')
    const dateStr = date.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric' })
    if (!timePart || timePart === '00:00') return dateStr
    const [h, m] = timePart.split(':').map(Number)
    const ampm = h >= 12 ? 'PM' : 'AM'
    const h12 = h % 12 || 12
    return `${dateStr}, ${h12.toString().padStart(2,'0')}:${m.toString().padStart(2,'0')} ${ampm}`
  }
  ```

## Technical Notes
- Use `Popover`, `PopoverTrigger`, `PopoverContent` from '@/components/ui/popover'
- Use `Calendar` from '@/components/ui/calendar'
- Use `Button` from '@/components/ui/button'
- `CalendarIcon` from 'lucide-react'
- `useState` for `open` (popover state) and parsing value into date/time parts
- Glass styling for popover: `glass-strong border border-white/20`
- react-day-picker Calendar's `selected` prop expects a `Date` object:
  ```ts
  const selectedDate = value ? new Date(value.split('T')[0] + 'T00:00:00') : undefined
  ```
- Tailwind v4 CSS-first: no config needed, glass classes already defined in globals.css

### UI Standard
- No standard defined — follow existing component patterns in the codebase.
  (Glass: glass-strong for popover; glass-surface for trigger; shadcn Button/Popover/Calendar primitives)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `npx shadcn add calendar` and `npx shadcn add popover` complete successfully
- [ ] `src/components/ui/calendar.tsx` exists
- [ ] `src/components/date-time-picker.tsx` exported as default
- [ ] Trigger button shows formatted date+time or placeholder
- [ ] Clicking trigger opens Popover with Calendar
- [ ] Calendar shows correct month, navigates with prev/next arrows
- [ ] Selecting a date updates the value (date part)
- [ ] Time input appears after date is selected
- [ ] Changing time updates the value (time part)
- [ ] Clear button sets value to null, closes popover
- [ ] Glass styling applied correctly in both light and dark mode
- [ ] No TypeScript errors

Created src/components/date-time-picker.tsx with DateTimePicker component featuring: glass-surface trigger button with CalendarIcon, Popover with glass-strong styling, shadcn Calendar for date selection, time input visible after date selection, clear button, and formatDisplay helper for 12-hour formatted display. TypeScript check passes clean.

description:

setSearchQuery(e.target.value)} className="pl-8 pr-8 glass-surface" aria-label="Search todos" autoComplete="off" /> {searchQuery && ( )}
) } ``` - Use `glass-surface` class on both the Input wrapper div and the sort Button (consistent with `todo-form.tsx` pattern) - Import pattern for icons: check lucide-react v0.575 — `Search`, `ArrowUpDown`, `ArrowUp`, `ArrowDown`, `X` are all available - `size='icon-xs'` is defined in `src/components/ui/button.tsx` variants ### UI Standard - No standard defined — follow existing component patterns in the codebase. title: Build root layout with ThemeProvider
description:
Set up the Next.js root layout with next-themes for dark mode support.

## Steps
1. Install: `npm install next-themes`
2. Create/update `src/app/layout.tsx`:

```tsx
import type { Metadata } from 'next'
import { ThemeProvider } from 'next-themes'
import './globals.css'

export const metadata: Metadata = {
  title: 'Todo',
  description: 'Modern todo app',
}

export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <html lang="en" suppressHydrationWarning>
      <body>
        <ThemeProvider attribute="class" defaultTheme="system" enableSystem>
          {children}
        </ThemeProvider>
      </body>
    </html>
  )
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ThemeProvider` wraps all children
- [ ] `attribute="class"` — Tailwind `dark:` classes work
- [ ] `defaultTheme="system"` — respects OS dark mode preference
- [ ] `suppressHydrationWarning` on `<html>` — prevents hydration mismatch
- [ ] `npm run dev` renders without React hydration warnings

## Technical Notes
### UI Standard
Follow brainstorm.md UI/UX decisions: glassmorphism card (backdrop-blur, semi-transparent bg), dark mode default via next-themes + Tailwind dark: classes. Reference: history/20260227-todo-app-nextjs16/brainstorm.md

- `suppressHydrationWarning` is required on `<html>` — next-themes dynamically
  adds the `dark` class, which causes a mismatch between server and client render
- `ThemeProvider` must exist BEFORE any component uses `useTheme()` —
  if ThemeToggle renders outside this provider, it throws
- Do NOT add `font-*` classes or Google Fonts imports in this bead unless
  explicitly in scope — keep layout minimal
close_reason: Updated src/app/layout.tsx with ThemeProvider from next-themes. suppressHydrationWarning on html, attribute='class', defaultTheme='system', enableSystem.

title: Analytics page — scaffold, overview cards, insights, streak
description:
# Analytics Page — Scaffold, Overview Cards, Insights, Streak ## Track: C — RedStone ## Scope: new `src/app/analytics/page.tsx`, new `src/lib/analytics.ts`, new `src/components/analytics/overview-cards.tsx`, new `src/components/analytics/insights-cards.tsx`, new `src/components/analytics/streak-tracker.tsx` ## Description Create the full analytics page at `/analytics` with server-side stat computation and three display sections. (Daily activity chart is added in C2.) ## Implementation ### `src/lib/analytics.ts` — Pure stat computation ```ts export interface AnalyticsStats { overview: { total: number completed: number active: number overdue: number completionRate: number // 0-100, rounded integer } dailyActivity: Array<{ date: string; created: number; completed: number }> // last 14 days insights: { mostProductiveDay: string | null // "Monday", "Tuesday", etc. avgCompletionDays: number | null // 1 decimal place oldestOpenTodo: { title: string; daysOpen: number } | null } streak: { currentStreak: number bestStreak: number last30Days: Array<{ date: string; count: number }> } } export function computeAnalytics(todos: typeof import('./schema').todos.$inferSelect[]): AnalyticsStats ``` **Overview:** - `overdue`: `completed === 0 && due_date && due_date < new Date().toISOString()` - `completionRate`: `total === 0 ? 0 : Math.round((completed / total) * 100)` **DailyActivity (last 14 days):** ```ts const today = new Date() for (let i = 13; i >= 0; i--) { const d = new Date(today) d.setDate(d.getDate() - i) const dateStr = d.toISOString().slice(0, 10) const created = todos.filter(t => t.created_at?.slice(0, 10) === dateStr).length const completed = todos.filter(t => t.completed === 1 && t.updated_at?.slice(0, 10) === dateStr).length dailyActivity.push({ date: dateStr, created, completed }) } ``` **Insights:** - `mostProductiveDay`: group completed todos by `new Date(t.updated_at).getDay()` (0=Sunday), find max day, format: `['Sunday','Monday','Tuesday','Wednesday','Thursday','Friday','Saturday'][maxDay]` - `avgCompletionDays`: for completed todos where both `created_at` and `updated_at` exist: `(new Date(t.updated_at).getTime() - new Date(t.created_at).getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24)`, take mean, round to 1 decimal - `oldestOpenTodo`: `todos.filter(t => t.completed === 0).sort by created_at asc`[0]; days = `(Date.now() - new Date(t.created_at).getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24)` rounded **Streak:** - Build a Set of dates with ≥1 completion: `completedDates = new Set(todos.filter(t => t.completed===1 && t.updated_at).map(t => t.updated_at.slice(0,10)))` - `currentStreak`: start from today, count backwards while date in set - `bestStreak`: iterate over sorted dates, track max consecutive run - `last30Days`: last 30 dates, each with count from a Map of date→count ### `src/app/analytics/page.tsx` (RSC) ```tsx import { db } from '@/lib/db' import { todos } from '@/lib/schema' import { asc } from 'drizzle-orm' import { computeAnalytics } from '@/lib/analytics' import { BarChart2 } from 'lucide-react' import OverviewCards from '@/components/analytics/overview-cards' import InsightsCards from '@/components/analytics/insights-cards' import StreakTracker from '@/components/analytics/streak-tracker' export default async function AnalyticsPage() { const allTodos = await db.select().from(todos).orderBy(asc(todos.created_at)) const stats = computeAnalytics(allTodos) return (

Analytics

{/* Daily chart placeholder — replaced in C2 */}
Daily activity chart (coming in C2)
) } ``` Match the gradient background from `src/app/page.tsx` exactly. ### `src/components/analytics/overview-cards.tsx` ``` Grid: grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-5 gap-3 ``` Each card (glass-card, rounded-xl, p-4): - Spectrum accent: `border-t-2 border-[var(--color-spectrum-N)]` (N=1..5) - Big number: `text-3xl font-bold` - Label: `text-sm text-muted-foreground mt-1` - Cards: Total (spectrum-1), Completed (spectrum-4/green), Active (spectrum-2/orange), Overdue (spectrum-7/red), Rate% (spectrum-6/blue) ### `src/components/analytics/insights-cards.tsx` ``` Grid: grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3 gap-3 ``` Cards with icon + value + label: - "Most Productive Day": `Trophy` icon + day name (or "—" if no data) - "Avg Completion Time": `Clock` icon + "X.X days" (or "—") - "Oldest Open Todo": `AlertCircle` icon + title (truncated, max 2 lines) + "X days open" subtext ### `src/components/analytics/streak-tracker.tsx` Layout: - Top row: Current streak (big Flame icon + number + "day streak") | Best streak (smaller) - Bottom: 30-day heatmap grid - `grid grid-cols-10 gap-1` (3 rows × 10 cols = 30 squares) - Each square: `w-7 h-7 rounded-sm` colored by count: - 0: `bg-muted/30` - 1-2: `bg-green-400/50` - 3+: `bg-green-500` - Tooltip on hover: show date + count (use `title` attribute for simple tooltip) ## Technical Notes - All computation in `src/lib/analytics.ts` — pure functions, no async - `today = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)` consistently - Background gradient: copy exact classes from src/app/page.tsx - Import `Flame`, `Trophy`, `Clock`, `AlertCircle` from 'lucide-react' ### UI Standard - No standard defined — follow existing component patterns in the codebase. (Glass: glass-card for cards; spectrum colors for accents; same container as main page.tsx) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `/analytics` page loads without errors - [ ] `src/lib/analytics.ts` exports `computeAnalytics` with correct TypeScript types - [ ] Overview cards: correct Total, Completed, Active, Overdue, Rate values - [ ] Overdue correctly counts only incomplete todos past their due_date - [ ] Insights: most productive day shown (or "—" if no data) - [ ] Insights: avg completion time shown (or "—") - [ ] Insights: oldest open todo shown with days count - [ ] Streak: current streak calculation correct - [ ] Streak: best streak correct - [ ] Streak: 30-day heatmap renders 30 colored squares - [ ] Glass container consistent with main page - [ ] Mobile responsive layout - [ ] No TypeScript errors

All glassmorphism styled with spectrum colors. Type check passes.

title: Configure Tailwind CSS v4 + shadcn/ui
description:
Set up Tailwind CSS v4 (CSS-first config) and initialize shadcn/ui with the new-york style.

## Steps
1. Delete any `tailwind.config.js` or `tailwind.config.ts` generated by scaffold
2. Update `src/app/globals.css`:
   - Replace `@tailwind base/components/utilities` with `@import "tailwindcss"`
   - Add `@import "tw-animate-css"` (replaces tailwindcss-animate)
   - Add `@theme` block with OKLCH color palette for shadcn
   - Add glassmorphism custom tokens (handled in bead 16; just establish structure here)
3. Install: `npm install tw-animate-css`
4. Run: `npx shadcn@latest init` — select:
   - Style: `new-york`
   - Color: `zinc` (or neutral — matches glassmorphism palette)
   - Tailwind CSS: Yes (v4 path)
5. Add required components: `npx shadcn@latest add button input checkbox tabs`
6. Verify `src/components/ui/` directory was created

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No `tailwind.config.js` in project root
- [ ] `globals.css` uses `@import "tailwindcss"` (v4 syntax)
- [ ] `src/components/ui/button.tsx`, `input.tsx`, `checkbox.tsx`, `tabs.tsx` exist
- [ ] `npm run dev` still compiles without errors after changes
- [ ] Dark mode classes (`dark:bg-*`) render correctly on manual toggle

## Technical Notes
### UI Standard
Follow brainstorm.md UI/UX decisions: glassmorphism card (backdrop-blur, semi-transparent bg), Framer Motion microinteractions (checkbox scale bounce, strike-through on complete, slide-out on delete via AnimatePresence), color-coded due dates (green=future, amber=today, red=overdue), shadcn/ui Tabs with count badges for filters, dark mode default via next-themes + Tailwind dark: classes. Reference: history/20260227-todo-app-nextjs16/brainstorm.md

- Tailwind v4 BREAKING: no `tailwind.config.js`; all config via `@theme` in CSS
- Use `tw-animate-css` NOT `tailwindcss-animate` (v4 incompatible)
- shadcn's CLI detects Tailwind v4 automatically on recent versions — use `npx shadcn@latest`
- OKLCH colors: shadcn v4 uses OKLCH instead of HSL — do not manually convert
- `forwardRef` removed from shadcn components in 2025 — no action needed on fresh init

title: Implement Drizzle schema + DB client
description:
Set up Drizzle ORM with the libSQL/Turso adapter and define the todos table schema.

## Steps
1. Install dependencies:
   ```bash
   npm install drizzle-orm @libsql/client
   npm install -D drizzle-kit
   ```
2. Create `src/lib/schema.ts`:
   ```ts
   import { integer, sqliteTable, text } from 'drizzle-orm/sqlite-core'

   export const todos = sqliteTable('todos', {
     id:         integer('id').primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
     title:      text('title').notNull(),
     completed:  integer('completed').notNull().default(0),  // 0 = false, 1 = true
     due_date:   text('due_date'),                           // ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DD or null
     created_at: text('created_at').notNull().default(sql`(datetime('now'))`),
     updated_at: text('updated_at').notNull().default(sql`(datetime('now'))`),
   })

   export type Todo = typeof todos.$inferSelect
   export type NewTodo = typeof todos.$inferInsert
   ```
3. Create `src/lib/db.ts`:
   ```ts
   import { drizzle } from 'drizzle-orm/libsql'

   const url = process.env.TURSO_DATABASE_URL!
   const authToken = process.env.TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN  // undefined OK for local file

   export const db = drizzle({ connection: { url, authToken } })
   ```
4. Add `.env.local` (if not already present): `TURSO_DATABASE_URL=file:./local.db`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `src/lib/schema.ts` exports `todos` table and `Todo` / `NewTodo` types
- [ ] `src/lib/db.ts` exports `db` instance using env-variable URL
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors on both files
- [ ] `completed` field typed as `integer` (0/1 boolean)

## Technical Notes
- `authToken` is optional for `file:./local.db` — do not throw if undefined
- `sql` import needed for `default(sql`(datetime('now'))`)`:
  `import { sql } from 'drizzle-orm'`
- This bead depends on bead 3 (next.config.ts) being complete — importing
  @libsql/client without serverExternalPackages crashes the dev server
- `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN` must NOT be committed to git — Vercel env vars only for prod
close_reason: Drizzle schema and DB client implemented. src/lib/schema.ts: todos table with id (integer PK autoincrement), title (text notNull), completed (integer default 0), due_date (text nullable), created_at (text), updated_at (text). src/lib/db.ts: drizzle-orm/libsql client using TURSO_DATABASE_URL env || 'file:./local.db'. drizzle-orm@0.43.1 and @libsql/client installed.

title: Build ThemeToggle component
description:
Build the dark/light mode toggle button using next-themes and lucide-react icons.

## Steps
1. Install lucide-react if not already present: `npm install lucide-react`
2. Create `src/components/theme-toggle.tsx`:

```tsx
'use client'
import { useTheme } from 'next-themes'
import { useEffect, useState } from 'react'
import { Moon, Sun } from 'lucide-react'
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'

export function ThemeToggle() {
  const { theme, setTheme } = useTheme()
  const [mounted, setMounted] = useState(false)

  useEffect(() => setMounted(true), [])

  if (!mounted) return <div className="w-9 h-9" />  // Prevent hydration mismatch

  return (
    <Button
      variant="ghost"
      size="icon"
      onClick={() => setTheme(theme === 'dark' ? 'light' : 'dark')}
      className="text-white hover:bg-white/10"
      aria-label="Toggle theme"
    >
      {theme === 'dark' ? <Sun className="h-4 w-4" /> : <Moon className="h-4 w-4" />}
    </Button>
  )
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Renders Sun icon in dark mode, Moon icon in light mode
- [ ] Click toggles between dark and light
- [ ] `mounted` guard prevents server/client hydration mismatch
- [ ] Placeholder `<div>` matches button size (no layout shift on mount)
- [ ] `aria-label` set for accessibility

## Technical Notes
### UI Standard
Follow brainstorm.md UI/UX decisions: dark mode default via next-themes + Tailwind dark: classes. Reference: history/20260227-todo-app-nextjs16/brainstorm.md

- `'use client'` required — `useTheme` is a client hook
- `mounted` guard is MANDATORY: without it, SSR renders one icon and client
  hydrates a different one → React hydration error
- `Button` variant="ghost" with `text-white hover:bg-white/10` blends with
  the glassmorphism dark background
- Component depends on `ThemeProvider` existing in layout (bead 8)

title: Add Framer Motion microinteractions to TodoItem
description:
Layer Framer Motion animations onto the functional TodoItem component for polished microinteractions.

## Steps
1. Install: `npm install framer-motion`
2. Update `src/components/todo-list.tsx` to ensure `AnimatePresence` is imported from `framer-motion`
3. Update `src/components/todo-item.tsx` to use optimized `m` components with `LazyMotion`:

```tsx
// In todo-list.tsx — wrap list with LazyMotion for bundle optimization
import { LazyMotion, domAnimation, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion'

// Wrap <ul> with LazyMotion:
<LazyMotion features={domAnimation}>
  <AnimatePresence initial={false}>
    {filtered.map(todo => <TodoItem key={todo.id} todo={todo} />)}
  </AnimatePresence>
</LazyMotion>

// In todo-item.tsx — replace <li> with motion.li (or m.li)
import { m } from 'framer-motion'

// Replace <li> with:
<m.li
  layout
  initial={{ opacity: 0, y: -10 }}
  animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
  exit={{ opacity: 0, x: -20, transition: { duration: 0.2 } }}
  transition={{ duration: 0.2 }}
  // ... existing className and handlers
>

// Animate checkbox bounce via whileTap on Checkbox wrapper:
<m.div whileTap={{ scale: 1.3 }} transition={{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 400 }}>
  <Checkbox ... />
</m.div>

// Animate title strike-through (use framer-motion layout transition):
<m.span
  layout
  className={`flex-1 text-sm text-white transition-opacity ${todo.completed ? 'line-through opacity-50' : ''}`}
>
  {todo.title}
</m.span>
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New todo items animate in (fade + slide down)
- [ ] Deleted todo items slide out left + fade (via `AnimatePresence` + `exit`)
- [ ] Checkbox tap has a spring scale bounce
- [ ] Completed title transitions smoothly to strike-through
- [ ] `LazyMotion` + `domAnimation` used (NOT full `motion` import) — bundle ~4.6kb not ~34kb
- [ ] Animations don't block interaction (`layout: false` on non-layout variants)
- [ ] No layout shift after animations complete

## Technical Notes
### UI Standard
Follow brainstorm.md UI/UX decisions: Framer Motion microinteractions — checkbox scale bounce, strike-through on complete, slide-out on delete via AnimatePresence. Reference: history/20260227-todo-app-nextjs16/brainstorm.md

- IMPORTANT: Use `m` + `LazyMotion` pattern, NOT `motion.li` directly — this
  keeps the feature JS to ~4.6kb vs the full ~34kb motion bundle
- `LazyMotion` provider belongs in `todo-list.tsx` wrapping `AnimatePresence`,
  NOT in `layout.tsx` — scoped to where animations are used
- `layout` prop on `m.li` enables smooth reordering when filter changes
- `initial={false}` on `AnimatePresence` prevents entrance animations on page load
- Avoid `layoutId` unless implementing shared element transitions (out of scope)
close_reason: Added Framer Motion microinteractions using LazyMotion+domAnimation+m components: slide-in/out with AnimatePresence in TodoList, checkbox bounce on toggle, animated strikethrough on complete, slide-out on delete

No activity data yet
} return (

Daily Activity (last 14 days)

} />
) ``` ### Replace placeholder in analytics page In `src/app/analytics/page.tsx`: - Remove the placeholder `
Daily activity chart (coming in C2)
` - Import and render `` ## Technical Notes - Recharts uses SVG rendering — no Tailwind conflict with v4 CSS-first - `'use client'` required on DailyActivityChart (Recharts is client-side) - ChartContainer from shadcn handles CSS variable color injection for dark mode - `var(--color-spectrum-1)` and `var(--color-spectrum-4)` are defined in globals.css @theme - ChartContainer uses `aspect-video` by default if no height set — override with `className="h-[280px]"` - `ResponsiveContainer` is handled internally by `ChartContainer` - Bar `radius={[4, 4, 0, 0]}` for rounded tops on bars ### UI Standard - No standard defined — follow existing component patterns in the codebase. (Glass-card wrapper; spectrum colors for bars; consistent with analytics page style) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `npx shadcn add chart` completes without errors - [ ] `src/components/ui/chart.tsx` exists - [ ] DailyActivityChart renders in the analytics page (replaces placeholder) - [ ] Two bar groups per day: Created (spectrum-1) + Completed (spectrum-4) - [ ] X-axis shows formatted date labels ("Mar 1", "Mar 2", etc.) for last 14 days - [ ] Y-axis shows integer count values - [ ] Tooltip appears on hover showing date + created count + completed count - [ ] Chart fills container width responsively - [ ] Dark mode colors resolve correctly - [ ] Empty state shown when all data is zero - [ ] No TypeScript errors

title: Harmonize TodoItem with glass-surface and replace inline SVG with Trash2
description:
## Context

TodoItem currently uses `bg-card` (a solid opaque background) inside the glassmorphism container, breaking the depth illusion. The delete button also uses an inline SVG instead of the lucide-react `Trash2` icon used elsewhere.

## What to Do

Edit `src/components/todo-item.tsx`:

**1. Replace `bg-card` with `glass-surface` in the `m.li` className:**

```tsx
// BEFORE (in m.li className)
"... rounded-lg border bg-card px-4 py-3 shadow-xs ..."

// AFTER
"... rounded-lg glass-surface border-transparent px-4 py-3 shadow-xs ..."
```

The full `m.li` className becomes:
```tsx
className="flex items-center gap-3 rounded-lg glass-surface border-transparent px-4 py-3 shadow-xs transition-colors hover:bg-accent/5 focus-within:ring-1 focus-within:ring-ring/30 outline-none list-none"
```

**2. Add `Trash2` to the lucide-react import:**
```tsx
// BEFORE
import { Pencil, Check, X } from 'lucide-react'

// AFTER
import { Pencil, Check, X, Trash2 } from 'lucide-react'
```

**3. Replace the entire inline SVG in the delete button with:**
```tsx
<Trash2 size={14} aria-hidden="true" />
```

## Visual Notes

- `glass-surface` is defined in globals.css: `background: var(--glass-bg-strong); backdrop-filter: blur(calc(var(--glass-blur) / 2)); border: 1px solid var(--glass-border);`
- In light mode: `var(--glass-bg-strong)` = `oklch(1 0 0 / 80%)` — semi-transparent white. If this looks washed out against the violet gradient, fallback to `bg-white/5 border-white/10`
- In dark mode: `var(--glass-bg-strong)` = `oklch(1 0 0 / 14%)` — subtle translucent layer
- `border-transparent` overrides the `border` from glass-surface to remove the visible border (keeping backdrop filter but removing the stroke)

## Technical Notes

- All existing Framer Motion animations (checkbox bounce, strikethrough scaleX, delete exit) are UNAFFECTED — only className changes
- Tailwind v4 CSS-first: `glass-surface` is a custom @layer utility, already defined in globals.css
- Do NOT import anything new except `Trash2`

## UI Standard

- Pattern: list view (todo items)
- Standard: inline — glass-surface items inside glassmorphism container for visual depth continuity

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `m.li` uses `glass-surface border-transparent` (not `bg-card border`)
- [ ] Delete button renders `<Trash2 size={14} />` (no inline SVG)
- [ ] `Trash2` added to lucide-react import
- [ ] All animations (bounce, strikethrough, delete exit) still work
- [ ] In dark mode: items appear semi-transparent against the container
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` passes
close_reason: Applied glass-surface styling to TodoItem m.li (replaced border bg-card with glass-surface border-transparent), added Trash2 to lucide-react import, replaced inline SVG delete icon with Trash2 component. All Framer Motion animations preserved.
